title: Cross-implementation conformance test vectors

artifacts.description: Valid HumanUniqueness circuit vectors with correct witness, public signals, and expected verification=true
Invalid HumanUniqueness vectors covering stale-root, revoked, expired, nonce-reuse outcomes with error codes
Valid AgentPolicy circuit vectors including cumulative bit encoding invariant cases
Invalid AgentPolicy vectors covering scope-superset and invalid cumulative bit encoding (bit 4 without bits 2+3)
Valid Delegation circuit vectors including phantom delegatee Merkle proof cases
Invalid Delegation vectors covering phantom-delegatee, scope-superset, expired, revoked outcomes
Edge-case vectors for root history buffer: root at position 29 (valid) vs evicted root at position 30 (invalid-stale-root)
JSON schema definition for a conformance vector: input_witness, public_signals, expected_result (bool), error_code (string|null), circuit, category
Canonical error code registry mapping each contract revert/error to a stable string identifier used across all vectors
Python test runner that loads all JSON vectors, generates proofs via snarkjs/circom, submits to contract, and asserts expected outcomes
TypeScript test runner (Hardhat/Mocha) for the same vector suite, enabling JS-ecosystem conformance verification
Interop test suite documentation: how to run, how to add vectors, coverage table by circuit and outcome category

steps: Define and document the canonical JSON schema for a conformance vector (input_witness, public_signals, expected_result, error_code, circuit, category) in VECTOR_SCHEMA.md
Enumerate all contract error codes and map them to stable string identifiers in ERROR_CODES.md
For HumanUniqueness: generate ≥10 valid vectors with correct witnesses and public signals
For HumanUniqueness: generate ≥10 invalid vectors covering stale-root, revoked, expired, nonce-reuse categories
For AgentPolicy: generate ≥8 valid vectors including cumulative bit encoding edge cases
For AgentPolicy: generate ≥8 invalid vectors covering scope-superset and malformed cumulative bits (bit 4 without bits 2+3)
For Delegation: generate ≥8 valid vectors including phantom delegatee Merkle proof scenarios
For Delegation: generate ≥8 invalid vectors covering phantom-delegatee, revoked, expired, and scope-superset outcomes
Generate root history buffer edge-case vectors: root at buffer position 29 (valid) and evicted root at position 30 (invalid-stale-root)
Implement the Python conformance runner that loads vectors, calls circom/snarkjs for proof generation, invokes contract, and asserts result and error code
Implement the TypeScript/Hardhat conformance runner using the same vector files
Run both runners against a local devnet to verify all vectors produce expected outcomes; fix any mismatches
Validate total vector count ≥60 and coverage across all 7 outcome categories for all 3 circuits
Write conformance/README.md with coverage table, run instructions, and contribution guide for new vectors
dependencies: IdentityOS contract ABI and deployed address (local devnet)
Root history buffer implementation (ring buffer, capacity 30)
Cumulative bit encoding spec for AgentPolicy scopes
snarkjs ≥0.7 for proof generation in both runners
Python web3.py + py_ecc for Python runner
